V-M-053.purpose: Verify that KrotPN creates, syncs, revokes, and reissues individual official MTProxy secrets safely and idempotently.

commands.command-1: cd backend && SECRET_KEY=test-secret-key-with-enough-length DATA_ENCRYPTION_KEY=dpFmfSVqfdAK3yx5MQV6Tcv35RZuw5MzaH0yhxJ01q0= PYTHONPATH=. ../.venv/bin/python -m pytest -o addopts='' tests/test_mtproxy_secret_sync.py tests/test_mtproto_provisioning.py tests/test_mtproto_admin_ops.py tests/test_scheduler.py -v

scenarios.scenario-1: Verified user receives one canonical official credential mode assignment and owner-only secure link.
scenarios.scenario-2: Unverified user receives no MTProxy secret and no runtime apply attempt.
scenarios.scenario-3: Active assignments render to a deterministic redacted manifest and apply only when the manifest changes.
scenarios.scenario-4: Admin revoke removes exactly one user secret and preserves unrelated active users.
scenarios.scenario-5: Admin reissue rotates exactly one user secret, invalidates the old runtime secret, and returns the new owner payload only to the owner.
scenarios.scenario-6: Startup replay after backend/runtime restart restores all active assignment secrets.
scenarios.scenario-7: Runtime apply failure returns degraded state and retries without blocking normal KrotPN login.

failure-policy.policy-1: Any raw secret in admin responses, audit events, logs, or smoke output fails verification.
failure-policy.policy-2: Any revoke/reissue operation affecting more than the target assignment fails verification.
failure-policy.policy-3: Any issuance before email verification fails verification.
